svg.text: AI-Enabled Multi-Modal Cardiac MRI Analysis for Early Detection and Risk Prediction of Heart Failure
Theoretical Framework — UCL PhD Research Proposal in Cardiovascular Science
Heart failure develops gradually — early intervention is critical
Early myocardial abnormalities remain clinically silent
Conventional diagnostics miss subtle pre-clinical changes
z = φ(𝒳) — Multi-modal inputs projected into shared latent space
ŷ = gω(z) — Predicted heart failure risk
Integration of multiple cardiac MRI tissue measurements (T1, T2, LGE, Cine)
Learning shared latent myocardial representations across modalities
Interpretable AI for imaging biomarker discovery (Grad-CAM, SHAP, Attention)
Pre-symptomatic prediction of heart failure risk from silent myocardial changes
